question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the domain_name values from grpcProxyEndpointJson returned by findNetworkNodes() to perform DNS cache poisoning against the gRPC proxy domains of >= 30% of network nodes, redirecting their gRPC traffic to attacker-controlled servers and effectively shutting down those nodes' consensus participation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a null fileId to findNetworkNodes() — since the parameter is typed as Long (nullable) — causing a NullPointerException or unexpected SQL behavior in the `where file_id = :fileId` clause of the latest_address_book CTE, crashing the mirror node service and depriving >= 30% of network nodes of their data feed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the stakingPeriod field returned by findNetworkNodes() to predict when staking periods end and time an attack on >= 30% of nodes to coincide with the staking period transition, causing those nodes to miss the staking period update and lose their staking eligibility without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an empty string as orderDirection to findNetworkNodes() — since the parameter is typed as String with no enum constraint at the repository interface level — causing both CASE branches in the ORDER BY clause to evaluate to null, resulting in undefined sort order that could cause pagination-based consumers to miss nodes and fail to monitor >= 30% of the network?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the startConsensusTimestamp and endConsensusTimestamp fields returned by findNetworkNodes() to determine the exact time window when the current address book is valid, and time a targeted attack on >= 30% of nodes to occur during an address book transition when nodes may be in an inconsistent state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly call findNetworkNodes() with varying minNodeId and maxNodeId values to perform a binary-search enumeration of all node IDs, then use the complete node map to identify and target the minimum set of nodes (>= 30%) needed to disrupt consensus without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the latest_node_stake CTE uses a correlated subquery `(select max(consensus_timestamp) from node_stake)` — which scans the entire node_stake table — by sending many concurrent requests to cause repeated full table scans that exhaust I/O bandwidth and crash the database, depriving >= 30% of network nodes of their mirror node service?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the memo field returned by findNetworkNodes() to extract operator-provided metadata about network nodes (e.g., geographic location, operator identity, infrastructure provider), and use that information to perform targeted physical or legal attacks against the operators of >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the description field returned by findNetworkNodes() to identify the software version or configuration details of network nodes, and use that information to exploit known version-specific vulnerabilities against >= 30% of nodes without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass limit=0 or limit=-1 to findNetworkNodes() — since the @Min(1) constraint is on NetworkNodeRequest but not enforced at the repository interface — causing the SQL `limit :limit` clause to behave unexpectedly (e.g., limit 0 returns no rows, limit -1 may return all rows in some DB configurations), leading to incorrect node enumeration that causes automated systems to shut down >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the complete node endpoint data (ip_address_v4, port, domain_name) from serviceEndpointsJson to perform a coordinated Slowloris or low-and-slow HTTP attack against the gRPC endpoints of >= 30% of network nodes, exhausting their connection limits without triggering brute-force detection?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the node_info CTE — which performs a full scan of the node table with no filtering — by sending many concurrent findNetworkNodes() requests to cause repeated full scans of the node table, exhausting database I/O and causing the mirror node to crash, depriving >= 30% of network nodes of their data synchronization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the stakeNotRewarded field returned by findNetworkNodes() to identify nodes with large amounts of unrewarded stake — indicating nodes that may be experiencing issues — and use that information to selectively target already-struggling nodes to push >= 30% of them offline?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a nodeIds array containing duplicate entries (e.g., [1L, 1L, 1L, ...] repeated thousands of times) to findNetworkNodes() to cause the `= any(:nodeIds)` clause to process a massive array with no deduplication, consuming excessive PostgreSQL memory and crashing the database, indirectly shutting down >= 30% of network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the publicKey field (hex-encoded with '0x' prefix) returned by findNetworkNodes() to perform public-key fingerprinting of network nodes, identify nodes using weak or deprecated key algorithms, and exploit those cryptographic weaknesses to impersonate or disrupt >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the three-CTE query structure of findNetworkNodes() — latest_address_book, latest_node_stake, node_info — by sending concurrent requests that each trigger three separate table scans, multiplying the database load by 3x per request and causing resource exhaustion that crashes the mirror node, depriving >= 30% of network nodes of their service?
